meta.title: Food & Nutrition
meta.subject: general
meta.description: Discover what different foods do for your body and how to eat well every day.

items.front: 🍎 Why do we need to eat fruits and vegetables?
items.back: Fruits and vegetables are packed with vitamins, minerals, and fibre. They protect us from illness, help us grow, and keep our skin and eyes healthy. Aim for 5 portions a day!
items.hint: They have vitamins and keep us healthy

items.front: 🍞 What are carbohydrates and why do we need them?
items.back: Carbohydrates (carbs) are the body's main source of energy. Foods like bread, rice, pasta, and potatoes are full of carbs. They fuel your brain and muscles!
items.hint: They give us energy to move and think

items.front: 🥩 What do proteins do for your body?
items.back: Proteins help build and repair muscles, bones, and tissues. Good sources include meat, fish, eggs, beans, and nuts. Growing children need plenty of protein!
items.hint: They build and fix muscles

items.front: 🧈 Are all fats bad for you?
items.back: No! Healthy fats (found in avocado, nuts, olive oil, and fish) are essential for brain health and absorbing vitamins. Unhealthy fats in junk food should be limited.
items.hint: Some fats are actually good for your brain

items.front: 💧 Why is water so important for your body?
items.back: About 60% of your body is water! You need water to digest food, carry nutrients, control body temperature, and keep your brain working. Drink 6–8 glasses daily.
items.hint: Your body is mostly made of it

items.front: 🥛 What does calcium do for your body?
items.back: Calcium builds strong bones and teeth. Dairy foods like milk, cheese, and yoghurt are rich in calcium. Leafy vegetables like broccoli also contain calcium.
items.hint: Strong bones and teeth need this

items.front: 🌾 What is fibre and why do we need it?
items.back: Fibre is found in fruits, vegetables, beans, and whole grains. It helps your digestive system work smoothly and keeps you feeling full longer. Good gut health means good health!
items.hint: It helps your digestive system

items.back: Too much sugar can cause tooth decay, weight gain, and energy crashes. Natural sugars in fruit are better than added sugars in sweets and fizzy drinks. Small amounts are fine!
items.hint: Too much causes tooth decay and energy crashes

items.back: A balanced diet includes: plenty of fruit & vegetables, starchy carbohydrates, some protein, some dairy (or alternatives), and small amounts of fats and sugars. Variety is key!
items.hint: Eating the right amounts of all food groups

items.front: 🐟 Why is fish good for you?
items.back: Fish, especially oily fish like salmon and mackerel, contain omega-3 fatty acids. These are amazing for brain development, heart health, and reducing inflammation in the body.
items.hint: Omega-3 in fish is great for your brain

items.question: Which nutrient gives your body the most energy?
items.explanation: Carbohydrates are the body's main and preferred energy source. Bread, rice, and pasta are good examples.
items.hint: Bread, rice, and pasta contain this

items.question: Which food group helps build and repair muscles?
items.explanation: Protein is essential for building and repairing muscles, skin, and other tissues. Meat, eggs, and beans are high in protein.
items.hint: Athletes eat lots of this to stay strong

items.question: Which mineral makes bones and teeth strong?
items.explanation: Calcium is essential for strong bones and teeth. Milk, cheese, and yoghurt are rich in calcium.
items.hint: Found in milk and dairy products

items.question: How many portions of fruit and vegetables should you eat each day?
items.explanation: Nutrition guidelines recommend at least 5 portions of fruits and vegetables every day for good health.
items.hint: Five-a-day!

items.question: Which of these is a healthy source of fat?
items.explanation: Avocados contain healthy unsaturated fats that are good for your heart and brain.
items.hint: A green fruit with good fats

items.question: What does fibre help your body do?
items.explanation: Dietary fibre helps food move through your digestive system and keeps your gut healthy.
items.hint: Think about your digestive system

items.question: Which drink is best for staying hydrated?
items.explanation: Water is the best drink for hydration with no added sugar or calories. Aim for 6–8 glasses a day.
items.hint: The simplest and purest drink

items.question: Which food is richest in iron?
items.explanation: Iron is found in red meat, lentils, spinach, and fortified cereals. Iron carries oxygen in your blood.
items.hint: Blood needs this to carry oxygen

items.question: What happens if you eat too much sugar?
items.explanation: Excess sugar causes tooth decay and can lead to weight gain and energy crashes.
items.hint: Bad for your teeth and waistline

items.question: Which oily fish is high in omega-3?
items.explanation: Oily fish like salmon, mackerel, and sardines are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which are great for brain health.
items.hint: A pink oily fish

items.question: What is a 'balanced diet'?
items.answer: Eating the right amounts of all food groups
options: Eating the right amounts of all food groups
Eating as much protein as possible
items.explanation: A balanced diet means eating a variety of foods from all food groups in the right proportions.
items.hint: Variety and proportion matter

items.question: Which vitamin does your body make from sunlight?
items.explanation: When sunlight hits your skin, your body produces Vitamin D, which helps absorb calcium for strong bones.
items.hint: You can 'soak it up' outside

items.question: What are the three macronutrients?
items.explanation: The three macronutrients are carbohydrates, proteins, and fats. They provide energy and are needed in large amounts.
items.hint: The big three nutrients — all give energy

items.question: Which vitamin prevents scurvy and is found in citrus fruits?
items.explanation: Vitamin C prevents scurvy and boosts the immune system. Citrus fruits, kiwis, and peppers are rich in Vitamin C.
items.hint: Found in oranges and lemons

items.question: Which nutrient is essential for healthy eyesight and found in carrots?
items.explanation: Vitamin A is vital for good eyesight, especially in low light. Carrots, sweet potatoes, and leafy greens are good sources.
items.hint: Carrots help you see in the dark because of this

items.question: What does BMR stand for in nutrition?
items.explanation: BMR is the number of calories your body needs to keep basic functions going at rest — breathing, heartbeat, temperature.
items.hint: Your body's resting energy needs

items.question: What is the difference between soluble and insoluble fibre?
items.answer: Soluble dissolves in water; insoluble does not
options: Soluble dissolves in water; insoluble does not
Insoluble is in fruits; soluble is in meat
Soluble is sugar; insoluble is starch
items.explanation: Soluble fibre (oats, beans) dissolves in water and lowers cholesterol. Insoluble fibre (wheat, vegetables) adds bulk to stools.
items.hint: Think of what happens to oats in water

items.question: How many calories are in 1 gram of fat?
items.explanation: Fat provides 9 calories per gram — more than double the 4 calories per gram from carbohydrates or protein.
items.hint: The most calorie-dense macronutrient

items.question: What is the role of probiotics in food?
items.explanation: Probiotics are live bacteria found in yoghurt, kefir, and fermented foods. They help maintain a healthy digestive system.
items.hint: Found in yoghurt — good for your gut

items.question: Which food is a complete protein, containing all essential amino acids?
items.explanation: Eggs are one of the few foods that contain all nine essential amino acids, making them a 'complete' protein source.
items.hint: A breakfast favourite with every amino acid
